sha256_inventory_temporarily_unread_pack.method: For each package directory: sha256sum of each file; PACKAGE_DIGEST = sha256 of sorted 'sha256sum path' lines for that package.
sha256_inventory_temporarily_unread_pack.note: Full per-file hash list recorded at freeze time in session inventory; package digests above bind package contents without reading prose.

independent_mechanism_reconstruction.statement: Reconstructed solely from design pack + issues #33/#34 + this contract, without reading econ redteam/breaker conclusions or importing their modules.
independent_mechanism_reconstruction.core_hypothesis: A finite new-ledger genesis pool can be allocated after a migration epoch by accepting claims that prove (i) source-chain control of an asset, (ii) payment of that asset to an exact genesis-committed charity script, and (iii) binding of a post-quantum destination key — all within one source transaction structure plus a PQ claim signature.
mechanism_objects: Immutable genesis charity set with exact script-byte destinations
Finite donation/migration epoch with cutoff and confirmations
Donation commitment binding chain, epoch, charity, vout, amount, PQ destination, nonce
Fixed integer genesis pool P of abstract quanta (not a BTC price)
Post-close proportional allocation of P by eligible satoshi weights

allocation_rule_normative.e_i: eligible_satoshis_i (positive integer weight for claim i)
allocation_rule_normative.remainder: R = P - sum_i allocation_i; never issued
allocation_rule_normative.supply_invariant: sum_i allocation_i + R = P and sum_i allocation_i <= P
allocation_rule_normative.ordering: claim rows ordered by nullifier_hex ascending

independent_mechanism_reconstruction.what_is_optimized_public_benefit_hypothe: Charity receives irreversible source-chain value; donor receives optional new-ledger allocation claim right proportional to eligible donated sats. Social benefit is hypothesized as charity net retained value plus migration/admission coordination — not token market price.
independent_mechanism_reconstruction.what_ledger_function_is_optimized: Deterministic, supply-conserving genesis distribution keyed to verified charity-bound donations; admission predicate fail-closed; not ongoing consensus.
explicitly_specified_functions: payment of source assets to charity scripts (required for eligibility)
fixed-pool proportional allocation after epoch close
PQ destination binding for claim right
silently_assumed_or_under_specified: Donor participation elasticity to expected allocation value
Charity rebate behavior and access frictions

product_function_bundle_for_comparisons.F1_charity_receipt: Source value transferred to immutable charity destinations
product_function_bundle_for_comparisons.F2_migration_binding: Cryptographic binding of donation to new-ledger claim/destination
product_function_bundle_for_comparisons.F3_finite_genesis_distribution: Deterministic finite pool distribution after epoch
product_function_bundle_for_comparisons.F4_permissionless_claim: No administrator approval of individual claims (given genesis parameters)
product_function_bundle_for_comparisons.optional_F5_transferable_economic_unit: NOT required by design text; often assumed in token discourse

expected_load_bearing_claims.claim: Integer floor-proportional allocation with unissued remainder never issues more than pool P.

Under pure pro-rata, splitting one identity's eligible sats across multiple nullifiers does not increase that identity's total floor allocation beyond unit-level floor effects (and may reduce it via extra remainders).
Concave weights (sqrt/log) without identity binding are Sybil-dominated: splitting increases total weight.
Per-identity caps require identity; without it, caps are Sybil-evaded. Cap-then-renormalize reallocates surplus to uncapped claimants.
Individual allocation share e_i/T is unknown until T freezes; late donors and miners can shock T near cutoff.
Charity rebates are out-of-protocol; crypto verifier can admit claims that are economically circular.
Cryptographic control ≠ legal clean title; mechanism admits stolen/tainted source if crypto predicates pass.
Transferability of allocation units is not required for F1–F4; non-transferable receipts or delayed transfer can serve migration recognition with less secondary-market attack surface.
Pro-rata by sats concentrates allocation and any proportional governance in large donors.
Models that hold total donations, participation, and rebate access fixed understate strategic responses and may reverse welfare conclusions.

assumptions_requiring_evidence: A1: Donors respond to expected allocation value (mercenary/rebate-seeking elasticity unknown).
A2: Charities' ability and willingness to rebate (access friction distribution unknown).
A3: Secondary-market price of allocation units (if transferable) — ungrounded without data.
A4: Share of additional vs displaced vs mercenary donations.
A5: Identity uniqueness feasibility under permissionless design.
A6: Governance rights attached to allocation (product decision, not design necessity).
A7: Opportunity cost of tainted funds for attackers (external markets, seizure risk).
A8: Miner/validator ability to influence cutoff inclusion (depends on real Bitcoin policy).
A9: Social preference weights on charity net vs donor surplus vs concentration.
A10: Legal treatment of donation-for-allocation (requires qualified counsel; not adjudicated here).
plausible_failure_modes: FM1: Transferable token becomes speculative contest; charity net eroded by rebates when access is easy.
FM2: Whale + colluding charity captures large share of pool and governance.
FM3: Concave weighting without identity fails via Sybil split.
FM4: Cap-without-identity fails via Sybil; identity requirement conflicts with permissionless claim.
FM5: Undersubscription: small T yields large allocation per sat; speculative coordination rushes in.
FM6: Oversubscription: allocation per sat tiny; only mercenary/tainted flow remains if EV high.
FM7: Denominator sniping / miner influence privileges late large donors.
FM8: Tainted funds laundered into 'clean' claim rights when EV of allocation exceeds retention cost.
FM9: Exchange omnibus aggregation centralizes claims and governance.
FM10: Simpler no-token direct donation dominates on F1 alone; transferability adds risk without necessary function.
FM11: Governance cap-then-renormalize still allows coalition capture via many sub-cap identities.
FM12: Floor remainder gaming in buggy reimplementations (design uses unissued remainder to prevent over-issue).

falsifiers_for_major_claims.falsifier: Exhibit integers P, {e_i} with sum floor(P*e_i/T) > P under the stated rule.

Exhibit split of e into e1+e2=e where floor(P*e1/T')+floor(P*e2/T') > floor(P*e/T) with T'=T (same total) — should fail for pure pro-rata when T fixed; note T may change if split changes claim count only with same total e.
Exhibit concave f with f(e1)+f(e2) <= f(e1+e2) for all positive e1,e2 (would contradict strict concavity of sqrt/log on positives).
Demonstrate a required product function in the design that cannot be achieved by non-transferable or delayed-transfer receipts while holding F1–F4 fixed.
Show an on-protocol field that detects and rejects all economically material rebates without off-chain data.
Show that welfare ranking of mechanisms is invariant under all admissible endogenous participation/rebate responses in the planned model family.

coverage: per-identity capped pro-rata + Sybil evasion without identity
rebate incidence with access friction parameter α
governance: proportional vs capped vs cap-then-renormalize
transferable vs delayed-transfer vs non-transferable vs no-token comparison holding F1–F4
combined attacks: whale+rebate, Sybil+gov, undersub+speculation, exchange timing
metrics: attacker_EV thresholds under declared price/cost assumptions
evidence_discipline: Label each result as mathematical / simulation under assumptions / structural pathway / unresolved
Do not call illustrative parameters realistic
Do not claim inevitability from possibility
Separate additional vs displaced vs mercenary flow as accounting identities requiring external data for magnitude

freeze_independence_statement: A freeze of unread economics packages and sibling audits reduces anchoring on prior econ conclusions and recommendation text, but does not prove perfect independence. Shared design documents, issue #33/#34 text, common economic vocabulary, and the same frozen subject commit remain common knowledge. This seat still reconstructs models independently in new code under the authorized audit directory.
post_freeze_authorized_inspection: historical issues, receipts, and commit ancestry belonging to the frozen subject
